methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 64.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Contec is a public company (KOSDAQ-listed) in the Space & Aerospace sector that has raised $58.7M to date. The valuation universe for Space & Aerospace comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median analysis was not applicable due to insufficient narrow comparables—while 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace broadly, zero candidates met simultaneous filters for stage, adjacency, and valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring also could not be applied; although the Space & Aerospace public median trades at 64.2x P/S across the 6-ticker universe, Contec's revenue is not on file, rendering a multiple-based valuation impossible without that input.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported from the available data. A credible analysis would require either Contec's disclosed revenue (to apply public multiples), or identification of at least three private comparables with reported valuations within a similar capital-raised band and operational maturity. The current dataset provides sector context but lacks the specific financial metrics necessary to establish quantitative value anchors. Additional due diligence on comparable funding rounds and revenue scale is prerequisite to valuation.
